item.description: GOP senators abandoned a planned vote on a proposed $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that would compensate Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump faces GOP resistance on compensation fund, ballroom money
media:content.media:description: GOP senators abandoned a planned vote on a proposed $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that would compensate Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

item.title: Trump faces GOP resistance on compensation fund, ballroom money

item.description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

media:content.media:title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41
media:content.media:description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
Ben Kamisar
item.description: The Democratic National Committee plunged into a fresh round of chaos Thursday after Chair Ken Martin was forced to release an autopsy report he commissioned about the failed 2024 presidential campaign

media:content.media:text: Ken Martin is partially obscured by a heavily-blurred foreground.
media:content.media:description: Ken Martin, chairman of the Democratic National Committee, following a press conference with Texas Democrats at the International Union of Painters and Allied Trades union hall in Aurora, Ill. on Aug. 5, 2025.

item.title: Pressure on DNC Chair Ken Martin builds amid questions over how he handled the 2024 autopsy

item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sullivan
item.description: GLP-1 drugs may be linked to a lower risk of cancer progression, according to new research that will be presented next week at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting.

media:content.media:text: Lung cancer MRI.
media:content.media:description: Lung cancer seen on an MRI scan.

item.title: GLP-1 drugs may reduce the risk of cancer progressing, study suggests

item.description: NBC News analyzed more than a thousand 911 calls from ICE facilities across the country showing a rise in self-harm and suicides. Homeland Security says it's simply because there are more people detained, something experts push back on. Julia Ainsley reports.

media:content.media:title: Suicides in ICE detention centers rise in past year as NBC News obtains 911 calls
media:content.media:description: NBC News analyzed more than a thousand 911 calls from ICE facilities across the country showing a rise in self-harm and suicides. Homeland Security says it's simply because there are more people detained, something experts push back on. Julia Ainsley reports.

item.title: Suicides in ICE detention centers rise in past year as NBC News obtains 911 calls

item.description: Two weeks after NBC News reported on coverage issues being had by beneficiaries of TriWest Healthcare Alliance, the company’s CEO has released a letter apologizing to the healthcare administrator’s 4.2 million beneficiaries. NBC News’ Erin McLaughlin reports in our series “The Cost of Denial.”

media:content.media:title: Healthcare CEO apologizes for coverage problems
media:content.media:description: Two weeks after NBC News reported on coverage issues being had by beneficiaries of TriWest Healthcare Alliance, the company’s CEO has released a letter apologizing to the healthcare administrator’s 4.2 million beneficiaries. NBC News’ Erin McLaughlin reports in our series “The Cost of Denial.”

item.title: Healthcare CEO apologizes for coverage problems

item.description: The Justice Department revealed criminal charges against 15 defendants over an alleged $90 million in Medicaid fraud in Minnesota. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

media:content.media:title: DOJ reveals criminal charges over $90 million in alleged Medicaid fraud in Minnesota
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department revealed criminal charges against 15 defendants over an alleged $90 million in Medicaid fraud in Minnesota. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

item.title: DOJ reveals criminal charges over $90 million in alleged Medicaid fraud in Minnesota

item.description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight

item.dc:creator: Claire Cardona
item.description: The family of one of the suspects in a deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque this week said his exposure to hateful and extremist content online “contributed to his descent into radicalized ideologies and violent beliefs.”The Vazquez family apologized for the actions Caleb Vazquez is accused of, which they said there was no excuse for, and spoke out against online spaces that “normalize hatred” in the statement provided to NBC San Diego by the family’s attorney

media:content.media:text: People take part in an early morning call to prayer at the Islamic Center of San Diego
media:content.media:description: The Islamic Center of San Diego reopened Wednesday following a shooting Monday that killed three people.

item.title: Family says suspect in San Diego mosque shooting was influenced by hateful content online

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
Ben Kamisar
item.description: The Democratic National Committee plunged into a fresh round of chaos Thursday after Chair Ken Martin was forced to release an autopsy report he commissioned about the failed 2024 presidential campaign

media:content.media:text: Ken Martin is partially obscured by a heavily-blurred foreground.
media:content.media:description: Ken Martin, chairman of the Democratic National Committee, following a press conference with Texas Democrats at the International Union of Painters and Allied Trades union hall in Aurora, Ill. on Aug. 5, 2025.

item.title: Pressure on DNC Chair Ken Martin builds amid questions over how he handled the 2024 autopsy

item.dc:creator: Gabe Gutierrez
Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: The president said that due to the Iran war and “other things,” he wasn’t sure if he’d be attending the nuptials of Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson this weekend.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump arrives for a "Rose Garden Club" dinner in honor of Police Week at the White House on May 11, 2026.

item.title: Trump says he’ll ‘try’ to attend son’s wedding this weekend but it’s ‘not good timing’

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Katherine Doyle
Frank Thorp V
item.description: Applications are already rolling into the Justice Department from hopefuls aiming for some of the nearly $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, even though the process can’t officially begin until commissioners are chosen to decide how the money is doled out.

media:content.media:text: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche Meets With Lawmakers On Capitol Hill Over President Trump's 1.8 Billion Compensation Fund
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

media:content.media:text: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., on Capitol Hill on April 14.
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

media:content.media:text: Former Trump campaign official Michael Caputo arrives on Capitol Hill in Washington
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Trump’s $1.8B fund isn’t officially open yet. That hasn’t stopped applications.

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen shares his top takeaway from President Donald Trump’s efforts this month to unseat fellow Republicans.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump in the Rose Garden at The White House on May 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump in the Rose Garden at The White House on May 11, 2026.

item.title: The biggest lesson from Trump’s retribution campaign: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sullivan
item.description: GLP-1 drugs may be linked to a lower risk of cancer progression, according to new research that will be presented next week at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting.

media:content.media:text: Lung cancer MRI.
media:content.media:description: Lung cancer seen on an MRI scan.

item.title: GLP-1 drugs may reduce the risk of cancer progressing, study suggests

item.dc:creator: Laura Strickler
Julia Ainsley
Helen Graham
item.description: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm

media:content.media:text: South Texas ICE Processing Center.
media:content.media:description: South Texas ICE Processing Center.

media:content.media:text: A Colorado Springs community group gathered outside City Hall on March 12, calling on elected officials to help secure the release of Colorado Springs mother Hayam El Gamal and her five children, who have been held in a federal detention center since June.
media:content.media:description: A Colorado Springs community group gathered outside City Hall on March 12, calling on elected officials to help secure the release of Colorado Springs mother Hayam El Gamal and her five children, who have been held in a federal detention center since June.

media:content.media:text: US-JUSTICE-IMMIGRATION
media:content.media:description: ICE agents look over lists of people's names and their hearing times and locations at the Federal Plaza courthouse in New York City before making arrests on June 27.

media:content.media:text: Victor Manuel Diaz's mother and son at his funeral.
media:content.media:description: Victor Manuel Diaz's mother and son at his funeral.

item.title: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Will Ujek
item.description: The Justice Department said the defendants defrauded Minnesota Medicaid and other programs of more than $90 million, and two of them billed for autism services that were never provided.

media:content.media:text: Colin McDonald gestures with both hands while speaking at a lectern. RFK Jr. is visible behind him.
media:content.media:description: Assistant Attorney General for the National Fraud Enforcement Division Colin McDonald addresses media to announce actions for combating fraud in Minnesota at the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Minnesota in Minneapolis, on May 21, 2026.

item.title: Minnesota autism, disabled services providers among 15 charged with Medicaid fraud

item.dc:creator: Jason Kane
Sara G. Miller
Erin McLaughlin
item.description: A contractor that administers the military’s healthcare benefits to more than 4 million people issued a broad apology to its beneficiaries this week for problems with its coverage

media:content.media:text: FORT WORTH, TEXAS - FEBRUARY 11, 2026: Guy Shoemaker, 64, poses for a portrait outside his home in Fort Worth, Texas, on Wednesday, Feb. 11, 2026. After his TRICARE plan transitioned to TriWest Healthcare Alliance, tens of thousands of dollars in previously approved claims for life-saving treatment and scans for his throat cancer were pulled back or denied because of a data error.
media:content.media:description: FORT WORTH, TEXAS - FEBRUARY 11, 2026: Guy Shoemaker, 64, poses for a portrait outside his home in Fort Worth, Texas, on Wednesday, Feb. 11, 2026. After his TRICARE plan transitioned to TriWest Healthcare Alliance, tens of thousands of dollars in previously approved claims for life-saving treatment and scans for his throat cancer were pulled back or denied because of a data error.

item.title: Military healthcare contractor apologizes to 4 million beneficiaries

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Sahil Kapur
Greg Rosenstein
item.description: We’re officially in the middle of an all-time great Western Conference finals. We’ll recap that, plus look ahead to Formula 1’s Montreal Grand Prix and continue our World Cup player countdown.

media:content.media:text: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander.
media:content.media:description: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander drives to the basket against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle during Game 2 of the NBA Western Conference finals in Oklahoma City.

media:content.media:text: George Russell.
media:content.media:description: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander drives to the basket against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle during Game 2 of the NBA Western Conference finals in Oklahoma City.

media:content.media:text: Mehdi Taremi.
media:content.media:description: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander drives to the basket against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle during Game 2 of the NBA Western Conference finals in Oklahoma City.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Thunder strike

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: For years, many concertgoers have voiced their frustration over how hard it is to get tickets to their favorite artists’ tours amid scalpers and soaring resale prices

media:content.media:text: The Spotify headquarters building on Jan. 23, 2023 in New York.
media:content.media:description: Spotify says it’s launching a program aimed at helping superfans of select artists get spots at concerts.

item.title: Spotify to reserve tour tickets for artist ‘superfans’ in support of live music

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: A white supremacist group faces a federal discrimination lawsuit filed by a real estate broker who says she was barred from purchasing property in their rural Arkansas community because of her Jewish ancestry, Black husband and biracial children

media:content.media:text: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas in Ravenden, Ark., in 2025.
media:content.media:description: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas at the Return to the Land settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in November. Orwoll and Thomas are among the defendants named in a discrimination suit.

media:content.media:text: Eric Orwoll at his "whites-only" settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in 2025.
media:content.media:description: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas at the Return to the Land settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in November. Orwoll and Thomas are among the defendants named in a discrimination suit.

item.title: Suit targets whites-only Arkansas community over applicant’s rejection for Jewish roots, Black husband

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
The Associated Press
item.description: Two-time NASCAR Cup Series champion Kyle Busch had been hospitalized with a severe illness, his family said Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Kyle Busch at Daytona International Speedway in 2024.
media:content.media:description: Kyle Busch at Daytona International Speedway in 2024.

item.title: Kyle Busch, 2-time NASCAR champion, dies at 41 after illness

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Brennan Leach
Scott Wong
item.description: Objections to President Donald Trump’s controversial “anti-weaponization” fund prompted Senate Republicans to postpone votes to fund ICE and the Border Patrol until June.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump speaks to members of the media near the ballroom construction site at the White House on May 19, 2026.

item.title: Republicans cancel votes amid GOP fight over Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Thursday that the city will use a lottery system to distribute 1,000 World Cup tickets at $50 apiece to residents of the five boroughs

media:content.media:text: Zohran Mamdani.
media:content.media:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani at City Hall on May 12.

item.title: Mamdani announces $50 World Cup ticket lottery for New York City residents

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: In NBC Select’s For What It’s Worth podcast, editors share the best and worst things to shop during 2026 Memorial Day sales. Plus, learn about saving tips they swear by.

media:content.media:text: Memorial Day is the first of numerous summer sales holidays, and knowing exactly what you should and shouldn't buy can help you shop strategically to save the most.
media:content.media:description: Memorial Day is the first of numerous summer sales holidays, and knowing exactly what you should and shouldn't buy can help you shop strategically to save the most.

media:content.media:text: Memorial Day is the first of numerous summer sales holidays, and knowing exactly what you should and shouldn't buy can help you shop strategically to save the most.
media:content.media:description: Memorial Day is the first of numerous summer sales holidays, and knowing exactly what you should and shouldn't buy can help you shop strategically to save the most.

item.title: How to shop Memorial Day sales the right way, according to experts

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Home Depot Memorial Day sales on tools, appliances, and gardening supplies from Dewalt, Miracle-Gro and LG.

media:content.media:text: Home Depot is a great retailer to shop over Memorial Day weekend if you are looking for tools, home improvement and appliances.
media:content.media:description: Home Depot is a great retailer to shop over Memorial Day weekend if you are looking for tools, home improvement and appliances.

media:content.media:text: Home Depot is a great retailer to shop over Memorial Day weekend if you are looking for tools, home improvement and appliances.
media:content.media:description: Home Depot is a great retailer to shop over Memorial Day weekend if you are looking for tools, home improvement and appliances.

item.title: The 39+ best Home Depot Memorial Day deals start at just $2
